title: quassia simaruba
intro: Quassia simaruba, also known as the quassia or yellow dogwood, is a deciduous tree native to Central and South America. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of quassia simaruba include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Quassia simaruba is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in the form of powder or resin, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare practitioner due to potential side effects.
